key_insight: Accurate exact-exchange treatment is needed to capture electronic localization that stabilizes monoclinic BiVO4 and explains its symmetry-breaking distortion.
engineering_relevance: The study links functional choice to crystal structure, band gap, and carrier masses, guiding more reliable screening and design of oxide photocatalysts.
method: Compare exchange-correlation functionals with and without spin-orbit coupling
Use dense Brillouin-zone sampling to compute band structures and effective masses
Analyze charge transfer between inequivalent oxygen sites to identify the distortion mechanism
evidence_signal: The abstract reports that the functional best reproducing the monoclinic structure also yields excellent band-gap predictions once excitonic and thermal effects are included.
watch_item: Results depend sensitively on the exchange-correlation approximation, so semi-local DFT may miss the ground-state phase and should not be used alone for phase-stability conclusions.
paper_title: Interplay between Electronic Structure, Chemical Bonding, and Lattice Symmetry in Bismuth Vanadate
